opinion.h: VOKES v. CITY OF CHICAGO, 377 U.S. 124 (1964)
VOKES ET AL. v. CITY OF CHICAGO. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF ILLINOIS. No. 855. Decided April 20, 1964.
opinion.p: Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
John C. Melaniphy, Sydney R. Drebin and Robert J. Collins for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
opinion.h: 378 REALTY CORP. v. NEW YORK RENT & REHAB. ADMIN., 377 U.S. 124 (1964)
opinion.p: 377 U.S. 124 378 REALTY CORP. ET AL. v. NEW YORK CITY RENT AND REHABILITATION ADMINISTRATION ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF APPEALS OF NEW YORK. No. 845. Decided April 20, 1964.
Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
Harris L. Present and Irving S. Freedman for appellants.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
